what_it_does: Defines a workshop-themed JSON story tree with a root tagline, three parent teaser nodes, and eight child page nodes carrying roles, archetypes, titles, copy, bullets, and figures.
observations: The meta block describes this as a specimen story-tree and explains that cuts are represented as nodes.
The root line is "We're Turning Raw Seams into Kept Work" and has three children labeled A, B, and C.
Parent nodes carry ids, titles, role arrays, and nested children.
Leaf nodes use role, archetype, title, and optional super, bullets, figure, or line fields.
The content stays within a neutral workshop vocabulary of seams, cutting, drawing, ledgers, seasons, and keeping records.
Numeric figures appear as structured objects for 9 seams and 365 days.
standouts: The tree encodes multiple presentation cuts through parent-child structure rather than separate summaries.
The metadata states a rule that any node or extracted fragment should read as complete at its own resolution.
The content combines narrative sequencing with layout archetype hints.
conventions: Top-level structure is meta plus root.
Parent nodes use roles arrays; leaf nodes use a single role string.
Leaf content varies by archetype through optional fields such as line, bullets, super, and figure.
Titles use chain-additive phrasing as described in the metadata.
concerns: The schema is implicit; field requirements for each archetype are not declared in the file.
Specification prose and story content are stored together, which may require consumers to distinguish guidance from renderable content.
notes: This file is a neutral workshop story fixture that encodes a hierarchical narrative and presentation structure.
questions: Are archetype values fixed to a known set outside this file?
Should parent roles always match the roles of their child nodes?
Is the meta block intended for runtime consumers, authoring guidance, or both?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the contents: the file presents a specimen story tree whose nodes remain complete at different cuts and resolutions.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to model reusable story content as a tree where each node can stand alone while also participating in larger page cuts.
novelty: The file treats teaser and full-tier versions as explicit nodes in one hierarchy, with metadata describing a compositional reading law.
contribution: Contributes a neutral, hierarchical story-content substrate with roles, layout archetypes, copy fields, and structured figures that a fused system would keep as an example of resolution-preserving narrative data.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON specimen story tree for workshop content, with a root tagline, teaser parents, full-page child nodes, roles, archetypes, bullets, lines, and figures.
intention_for_embedding: This was for demonstrating a reusable interchange object where narrative cuts are represented as complete nodes at multiple resolutions.
